text: On 10 November 2024, 7 individuals suspected of being from the Logir community from Ikotos County attacked a civilian at his farm on the outskirts of Chukudum Town (Budi, Eastern Equatoria), killing him. The suspects then moved towards Kidepo Valley of Ikotos County. Motivation unknown/not reported. The deceased is likely a Didinga community member who reside in the area.

text: On 27 October 2024, armed individuals suspected to be Buya/Boya community members shot at a vehicle belonging to a road construction company between Lobira in Ikotos County and Tuhubak in Torit County along the Torit-Kapoeta Highway location coded to Lobira (Huma boma), (Ikotos County, Eastern Equatoria State). The incident left a soldier from the Internal Security Bureau (ISB) of the National Security Service (NSS) dead while a Sudanese national, was injured. The victims were returning from an assessment of the Juba-Torit-Kapoeta-Nadapal Highway for an upcoming construction, the deceased was among officers escorting the group. Motivation unknown/not reported.

text: On 26 October 2024, youth from Kapoeta South County (presumed to be from the Toposa community) clashed with local youth (likely from the Didinga community based on location) in Marira Boma of Ngauro/Lauru Payam (Budi, Eastern Equatoria). The clash followed an attempted a cattle raid by youths from Kapoeta South County at a cattle camp, 3 persons were killed and 3 others were injured. A local chief disputed the fatalities noting that only 1 individual was killed.

text: On 22 September 2024, assailants, suspected to be youth from Ngauro Payam (coded as an unidentified armed group due to the area's diverse clans), stabbed, shot and killed an SSPDF soldier who was burning charcoal in Kimotong, Kimotong Payam (Budi county, Eastern Equatoria state). The motive remains unknown/unreported. The soldier's gun was stolen.

text: On 26 August 2024, raiders from Logir in Ikotos county, described as bandits, were pursued by local youths (coded as an unidentified clan militia) and clashed at Chukudum in Komiri (Budi county, Eastern Equatoria state) after a cattle raid. One raider was killed. An unspecified number of cattle were raided and later recovered. Four granaries/stores were burnt.
